nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: FastMCP server that registers seven MCP tools: four for local log files (tail, search, filter_level, summarize) and three for Docker container logs (docker_logs, docker_grep, docker_errors). Delegates all logic to the file_logs and docker_logs tool modules.

nodes.name: file_logs.py
nodes.summary: Log analysis functions for local files. Supports tailing, regex search, level filtering (handles both JSON-structured logs and plain text), and error summarization with pattern deduplication via Counter.

nodes.name: docker_logs.py
nodes.summary: Log analysis functions for Docker containers using the docker-py SDK. Mirrors the file_logs API with docker_tail, docker_search, and docker_summarize_errors, normalizing timestamps and hex IDs to group error patterns.

nodes.name: test_docker_logs.py
nodes.summary: Pytest tests for docker_logs tools using mocked docker-py client.

nodes.name: test_file_logs.py
nodes.summary: Pytest tests for file_logs tools using temporary log files.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Project configuration declaring dependencies (fastmcp, docker) and the console entry point for log-mcp-server.

nodes.name: tail_logs
nodes.summary: Returns the last N lines from a log file on disk.

nodes.name: search_logs
nodes.summary: Searches a log file with a case-insensitive regex and returns matching lines up to max_results.

nodes.name: filter_by_level
nodes.summary: Filters log lines by severity level, detecting both JSON-structured logs (level/severity/lvl keys) and plain text log lines.

nodes.name: summarize_errors
nodes.summary: Groups and counts error lines by normalized pattern (strips timestamps and hex IDs) and returns the top N error patterns.

nodes.name: docker_tail
nodes.summary: Fetches the last N log lines from a Docker container via the docker-py SDK.

nodes.name: docker_search
nodes.summary: Searches Docker container logs using a regex pattern, returning matching lines.

nodes.name: docker_summarize_errors
nodes.summary: Fetches and groups error/exception/fatal lines from a Docker container's logs into a ranked pattern summary.

steps.reason: Start here to see the four MCP tools registered for local file log analysis.
The most interesting tool — handles both JSON-structured and plain-text logs with a single function.
See how error patterns are normalized and deduplicated to surface the most frequent issues.
All Docker log analysis lives here — uses docker-py SDK to fetch logs from running containers.
See the error normalization pattern: strips timestamps and hex IDs before counting.
Tests demonstrate how the docker client is mocked for unit testing.
